lede: A look at June 29 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Giorgio Napolitano and Pedro Montt, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Apple Inc.
events.desc: Apple Inc. releases its first mobile phone, the iPhone.

events.title: Vincent van Gogh
events.desc: Vincent van Gogh's painting, the Le Pont de Trinquetaille, is bought for $20.4 million at an auction in London, England.

events.title: Seychelles
events.desc: The Seychelles become independent from the United Kingdom.

events.title: Supreme Court of the United States
events.desc: The United States Supreme Court rules in the case Furman v. Georgia that arbitrary and inconsistent imposition of the death penalty violates the Eighth and Fourteenth Amendments and constitutes cruel and unusual punishment.

events.title: Soviet Union
events.desc: Prior to re-entry (following a record-setting stay aboard the Soviet Union's Salyut 1 space station), the crew capsule of the Soyuz 11 spacecraft depressurizes, killing the three cosmonauts on board. Georgy Dobrovolsky, Vladislav Volkov and Viktor Patsayev are the first humans to die in space.

events.title: Korean War
events.desc: Korean War: U.S. President Harry S. Truman authorizes a sea blockade of Korea.

events.title: Hyde Park Township, Illinois
events.desc: Hyde Park and several other Illinois townships vote to be annexed by Chicago, forming the largest United States city in area and second largest in population at the time.

events.title: 1764 Woldegk tornado
events.desc: One of the strongest tornadoes in history strikes Woldegk, Germany, killing one person while leveling numerous mansions with winds estimated greater than 300 miles per hour (480 km/h).

events.title: Globe Theatre
events.desc: The Globe Theatre in London, built by William Shakespeare's playing company, the Lord Chamberlain's Men, burns to the ground.

events.title: Jacques Cartier
events.desc: Jacques Cartier is the first European to reach Prince Edward Island.

births.name: Giorgio Napolitano
births.line: Italian journalist and politician, 11th President of Italy

births.name: Pedro Montt
births.line: Chilean lawyer and politician, 15th President of Chile

births.name: Robert Schuman
births.line: Luxembourgian-French lawyer and politician, Prime Minister of France

deaths.text: Abel, King of Denmark, King of Denmark from 1250 to 1252

observances.text: Christian feast day: Mary, mother of John Mark
Christian feast day: Feast of Saints Peter and Paul (Western Christianity), and its related observances: Haro Wine Festival (Haro, La Rioja)
Christian feast day: Feast of Saints Peter and Paul (Western Christianity), and its related observances: l-Imnarja (Malta)
Independence Day (Seychelles), celebrates the independence of Seychelles from the United Kingdom in 1976.
